Career Advice 　>　 Cover Letters

Cover Letters

Need a cover letter for your CV? No matter what type of cover letter advice you’re looking for, we’ve got you covered. From graduate cover letter templates and career change cover letter templates, right through to free cover letter templates – along with a whole host of additional tips, you’ll find everything you need to write the perfect job application letter.

How to read a job description (and use it to apply)

Learn how to decode job descriptions, uncover what employers really want, and use those clues to write a stronger application.

Redundancy cover letter template

Been made redundant? Don’t panic. Here is our free redundancy cover letter template and tips on how to explain your situation to employers.

Career change cover letter template

Looking to switch sectors? Download our free career change cover letter template and learn how to showcase your transferable skills.

Speculative cover letters: What you need to know

Want to work for a company that isn’t hiring? Learn how to write a speculative cover letter and uncover the hidden job market.

How to avoid common cover letter mistakes

Are you making these seven cover letter mistakes? From oversharing to forgetting to tailor it, learn what to avoid to ensure you stand out to employers.

Retail Assistant cover letter template

Writing a retail cover letter? Stand out from the crowd with our free retail assistant cover letter template and expert writing tips.

Writing a retail cover letter? Stand out from the crowd with our free retail assistant cover letter template and expert writing tips.

Learn how to write a graduate cover letter with our expert guide. Includes a free template, examples, and tips to land a graduate job.

What is a cover letter?

Confused about cover letters? We explain exactly what they are, why they matter, and what they should always include.

Part-time cover letter template

Learn how to write a cover letter for a part-time job that will impress recruiters. Our guide includes a free template and expert tips.

Career break cover letter template

Returning to work after a break? Our free career break cover letter template will help you explain your gap and land your next job.

Retail cover letter template

Writing a cover letter for a retail job? Our free template will help you impress hiring managers and land your next role in retail.

School leaver cover letter template

Writing your first cover letter? Our free template will help you highlight your skills and land your first job after leaving school.

Cover letter examples and samples

To help give your cover letter a sense of structure, here are some cover letter examples that might inspire you.

Not heard back from an employer? Here’s when and how to follow up on a job application – plus some email templates to help you get started.

Feel like your cover letter could do with some work? Here’s our handy free cover letter template to help get you started.

How to: Write a cover letter

Behind every CV is a good cover letter. Here’s our step-by-step guide to help you make yours stand out.

More articles

FAQs

What is a cover letter?

A cover letter is a document you send with your CV when applying for a job. It’s your chance to introduce yourself to the hiring manager, explain your interest in the role, and highlight the specific skills and experiences that make you the perfect fit. Think of it as your professional sales pitch – and a way to stand out from the other applicants.

How do I write a cover letter?

To write a great cover letter, start with a strong opening that grabs the reader’s attention. Then, use the main body to match your skills and experience to the job description, providing specific examples. For step-by-step advice, check out our guide on how to write a cover letter. And if you hit a hurdle, cover letter help has extra tips to enhance your application.

What is the best format for a cover letter?

The best cover letter format is clean, professional, and easy to follow. Aim for three or four short paragraphs: an introduction, a body explaining your suitability, and a conclusion. For a clearer idea, try our free cover letter template or use our various cover letter templates to get started.

Do I always need to include a cover letter?

While not every application explicitly asks for one, sending a cover letter is almost always a good idea. It shows you’re willing to take the extra step and is your secret weapon for landing interviews. If you need examples, check out our cover letter examples and samples for inspiration.
